graveyard: Brain rot (the term, not the condition)

trends.name: Dustin Lucas Will Mike (Stranger Things rap recap)

def.normie: A meme based on a 2025 Jimmy Fallon segment where the Stranger Things cast performed a recap of the show in rap form. Creators have been remixing and exploiting the audio since early 2026, and by mid-June it fused with the 'Withered Flight' analog-horror FlightReacts edits for an uncanny mashup.
def.normal: The Fallon-stage rap recap from late 2025 finally completed its meme arc this week — combined with Withered Flight, the format is now an exploitable canvas for any 'announce who you are' POV.
def.zoomer: Will Mike and the boys are now a SOUND. Pair it with Withered Flight and you've got the meme of the week. Brain melting in real time.

trends.origin: Stranger Things cast on Jimmy Fallon, late 2025 → TikTok exploitable, June 2026
trends.vid: dustin lucas will mike stranger things tonight show

def.normie: An analog-horror style image macro using a distorted, uncanny edit of YouTuber FlightReacts. The edit became popular as the visual half of the Dustin Lucas Will Mike meme cycle.
def.normal: Mid-June analog-horror edit of FlightReacts that paired up with the Stranger Things rap recap. Either half works alone; together they're undefeated.
def.zoomer: Withered Flight x Dustin Lucas Will Mike is the ungovernable mashup the timeline deserved. Pure 2026 brain chemistry.

trends.name: House of the Dragon S3 return discourse

def.normie: House of the Dragon Season 3 returned on June 21, 2026 after a long break. Fans are back to live-tweeting episodes, posting theories, and arguing about the show's pacing — driving a huge volume of edits and reaction content.
def.normal: Premiere-day discourse: live-tweet flurry, theory threads, hot takes about the season-opener cliffhanger. Edits set to PRESSURE and Game Time already flooding the FYP.
def.zoomer: S3 dropped today and the timeline is unhinged. Cliffhanger discourse > actual cliffhanger. Cast getting edited to literally every audio on this board.

trends.vid: house of the dragon season 3 premiere

trends.name: "Du bist gut genug" Cleveland Brown Jr. edits

def.normie: An audio clip in German meaning 'you are good enough' (sung in falsetto by Rayan Djima) became a viral sound on TikTok in early June. Creators pair it with cartoon-character mashups, most popularly clips of Cleveland Brown Jr. from The Cleveland Show, for unexpectedly emotional comedy.
def.normal: Rayan Djima's wholesome-affirmation falsetto + animated-character mashups (Cleveland Jr. is the breakout) collided into a format that's somehow earnest and surreal at once. Saturation alarm is now blinking.
def.zoomer: Cleveland Jr. being told he's gut genug by a German voiceover is still rewiring brain chemistry. We're now in 'parody of the parody' phase — give it three more days.

trends.origin: Rayan Djima → TikTok audio, early June 2026
trends.vid: du bist gut genug rayan djima cleveland brown

trends.name: "PRESSURE!" by Nyck Caution (WC audio)

def.normie: Brooklyn rapper Nyck Caution's track 'PRESSURE!' is still the dominant sound for World Cup highlight reels and clutch-moment edits — now also colonising House of the Dragon S3 fan-edits.
def.normal: Tournament-fuelled audio breakout, now expanding to HOTD S3 premiere reactions. Sports edits first, drama edits second, gym/exam-season tier-three.
def.zoomer: PRESSURE! has officially escaped the football niche. Targaryen edits, finals-week edits, Slack-notification edits — all PRESSURE all the time.

trends.origin: Nyck Caution single → TikTok WC soundtrack wave, June 2026
trends.vid: pressure nyck caution world cup tiktok

def.normie: A format where dramatic saxophone music starts quietly and gets louder as a situation gets worse.
def.normal: The escalation-saxophone format: music swells as the on-screen situation collapses. Past peak; the brand-account flood arrived right on schedule.
def.zoomer: The sax has reached the 'corporate Instagram uses it unironically' phase. Cooked but immortal.

trends.name: Red Card Confusion (World Cup MEX vs SA)

def.normie: During the FIFA World Cup 2026 opener at Mexico City's Azteca Stadium, referee Wilton Sampaio gave three red cards in the Mexico vs. South Africa match. The South African players' bewildered reactions became the tournament's first viral meme.
def.normal: World Cup opener at Azteca, three red cards in one match, and the SA bench's 'wait, what?' faces. Format still being recycled but the original-clip cycle is past peak.
def.zoomer: Still appearing in everyone's POVs but the original SA-bench clip is now reference, not source. Tournament has new things now.

trends.origin: Mexico vs. South Africa, FIFA World Cup 2026 opener — June 12, 2026
trends.vid: red card confusion mexico south africa world cup

trends.name: Olivia Rodrigo "sad for a girl so in love" lyric overlays

def.normie: Olivia Rodrigo released an album called 'you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love' on June 12. Creators are pairing the lyrics with text overlays about relationships, friendships, and life moments.
def.normal: Album-drop lyric-overlay wave; the 'sad for a girl so in love' line is still doing the most work but other album cuts are eating it from below. Brand-account week underway.
def.zoomer: Olivia's lyrics now in every situationship post AND every Target ad. The Reset crowd has officially called it cooked but stans are stans.

trends.vid: olivia rodrigo sad for a girl so in love lyric trend

trends.name: "no no no NO NO NO"

def.normie: A short audio clip where someone says 'no no no' quietly and then yells 'NO NO NO' as panic sets in.
def.normal: The 'I Green Screen Things' escalating-no audio. Still load-bearing on panic-edits; now in 'reaction-stitch' phase as creators stitch their own reactions to the original.
def.zoomer: Still undefeated for any pre-crashout moment. The format mutated into stitched reactions and that's keeping it alive.

trends.origin: I Green Screen Things audio, TikTok 2026

trends.name: Nihilistic Penguin

def.normie: Old footage of a penguin walking alone toward the mountains instead of the sea.
def.normal: Werner Herzog's 2007 documentary penguin became 2026's mascot for opting out. Now in LinkedIn-influencer-overuse phase — but still has months of half-life left.
def.zoomer: Penguin is now a LinkedIn motivational poster. The exact death we predicted. Still a banger and will outlive everything else on this board.

trends.origin: 'Encounters at the End of the World' (2007) → meme, 2026

def.normie: 'AI slop' is the term for cheap, mass-produced AI content clogging up feeds.
def.normal: Backlash discourse is past peak but the term itself is now baseline vocabulary across every commentary feed.
def.zoomer: AI slop went from discourse to permanent vocab. The fight isn't over, it's just normal now.

def.normie: On June 16, TikTok creator @yowheredidjogo posted a video posing for various 'fairs' stickers. Other creators copied the format — striking exaggerated poses meant to be screen-grabbed and used as reaction stickers.
def.normal: The 'fairs' format is reaction-image-as-performance. Brand attempts confirmed this weekend, accelerating the slide.
def.zoomer: Born Jun 16, eaten by Jun 18, brand-coopted by Jun 20. Textbook 2026 lifecycle. Word 'fairs' will outlive the format.

def.normie: A pop song by British singer-songwriter Sienna Spiro from The Devil Wears Prada 2 soundtrack. Creators are still using it on fashion hauls and outfit checks, but the brand-account flood has officially arrived.
def.normal: Soundtrack drop turned algorithm gift that's now finishing its mainstream-saturation arc.
def.zoomer: It's in a Target ad now. Confirmed cooked. Use ironically only.

trends.name: KitKat Heist memes

def.normie: Memes about the real-life European theft of around 400,000 KitKat bars.
def.normal: Past peak; now in reference-meme zone. Used as a vibe descriptor, no longer as a joke.
def.zoomer: KitKat heist now means 'audacious heist energy.' The cinematic universe is canon, the meme is reference.

trends.origin: Real European logistics theft → meme, mid-2026

trends.name: I am my perimeter

def.normie: A streamer's deadpan 'I am my perimeter and my perimeter is me' became a catchphrase.
def.normal: Snapiyya's Rainbow Six Siege line is now a load-bearing 'locked in' declaration in corporate Slack. Slowly fading from social usage as a result.
def.zoomer: When your boss starts saying it, it's officially cringe. Approaching that phase fast.

trends.origin: Snapiyya / Rainbow Six Siege stream clip, 2026

def.normie: A short video format that cuts from 'sorry I can't today, I have therapy' to whatever the creator actually does to feel better.
def.normal: Reels POV format past peak. Brand accounts now overusing the cut, which is the death rattle.
def.zoomer: Bro said 'I have therapy' and the next shot is a corporate ad. We are in the bad ending.

trends.name: "Game Time" / FIFA WC 2026 audio

def.normie: Future and Tyla's song 'Game Time' from the official FIFA World Cup 2026 soundtrack.
def.normal: Lost the head-to-head with PRESSURE! Still ubiquitous on official broadcaster content but the algorithmic crown went elsewhere.
def.zoomer: PRESSURE! ate it. Game Time is now the 'safe' WC audio brands use when they don't want to seem too online.

trends.vid: game time future tyla fifa world cup trend

def.normie: An insult for robots, AI, or people who rely on AI too much — borrowed from Star Wars.
def.normal: Now squarely mainstream and slightly cringe to use sincerely. Permanent vocab — but you're tipping yourself off as Online any time you say it.
def.zoomer: Dad-zone confirmed. Still works but use sparingly or you're aging yourself.

def.normie: To completely lose your cool — a meltdown, usually public.
def.normal: Permanent vocabulary. The term outlives every cycle.
def.zoomer: It's in your principal's PowerPoint by now. Permanent. Use forever.

trends.name: Homer Drops His Donut (AI song)

def.normie: An AI-generated song where Homer Simpson 'sings' his 'D'oh!' over every minor inconvenience.
def.normal: The Slop That Worked is now the Slop That's Tired. The anti-AI-slop crowd finally got their wish.
def.zoomer: AI Homer has been usurped. Withered Flight ate his lunch.

trends.vid: homer drops his donut ai song

trends.name: "Remember who you are" / Freakin' Out

def.normie: Reels trend pairing 'Freakin' Out' by Dexter and the Moonrocks with the phrase 'remember who you are' over travel and personal-growth montages.
def.normal: Now in parody phase. The earnest version is officially dated; the parodies are the only fresh use.
def.zoomer: Saying 'remember who you are' straight-faced now reads as a self-own. Use ironically.

trends.origin: Dexter and the Moonrocks 'Freakin' Out' → Reels trend, 2026
trends.vid: remember who you are reels trend

def.normie: A movement calling for a return to older, simpler human-made internet humour.
def.normal: The Reset got what it wanted — Dustin Lucas Will Mike and Withered Flight are exactly the kind of human-made nonsense it preached. Discourse cooling now that the wins are visible.
def.zoomer: The Reset won. We have organic meme chaos. The Reset crowd can now go outside.

trends.name: Red vs. Blue Button dilemma

def.normie: A viral moral-dilemma post: 'Press the red button and X happens; press the blue button and Y happens.'
def.normal: Past peak — every niche has done its version, debates have run their course. Reference-meme phase.
def.zoomer: Red blue button is now just trolley-problem fanfiction. Used, abused, done.

trends.vid: red blue button moral dilemma meme

trends.name: "Hate That I Made You Love Me" mashup

def.normie: A mashup using Ariana Grande's song 'Petal' that creators jumped on through early June.
def.normal: Officially yesterday's news — Olivia ate it, then PRESSURE! and Du bist gut genug both walked over the corpse.
def.zoomer: Reference only. RIP.

trends.vid: hate that i made you love me trend

trends.name: Beez in the Trap × What's Up? mashup

def.normie: A mashup blending Nicki Minaj's 'Beez in the Trap' with 4 Non Blondes' 'What's Up?'.
def.normal: Cooked. The 'remember when' phase. Brand-coopted, retired.
def.zoomer: In the meme history book now. RIP, godspeed.

trends.vid: beez in the trap whats up mashup

def.normie: A way to call someone empty or absent — like the '404 page not found' error.
def.normal: The '-coded' suffix found its tech-meme breakout in mid-2026, then aged out by late June.
def.zoomer: Retired vocab. If you still say it sincerely you're 404 coded yourself.

def.normie: The joke that consuming too much silly internet content rots your brain — Oxford's 2024 word of the year.
def.normal: Universally understood, no longer cutting edge. The term is permanent vocab now — fully mainstream so it's no longer a trend.
def.zoomer: Brain rot is a census statistic. Not a flex, not a trend. Just a fact of life.

def.normie: X rolled out a 'Certified Banger' badge for viral posts and the internet immediately fought about it.
def.normal: X's viral-post badge launched first week of June and became the discourse for a week. Now just a platform feature, the meme has fully evaporated.
def.zoomer: The badge is just a button now. The cope outlasted the discourse, as it should.

def.normie: Fan theory claiming the Stranger Things finale was an illusion.
def.normal: The viral theory that the Stranger Things finale concealed a hidden episode. Speedrun complete, archived as tradition.
def.zoomer: Closed canon. The theory still bubbles up but the cycle is over.

def.normie: A mash-up of 'chopped' (unattractive) and 'doppelganger': an unflattering lookalike.
def.normal: Format dead, word survives in conversation only.
def.zoomer: Use in DMs, not on camera.

def.normie: An AI-generated universe of surreal characters with nonsense Italian names (Tralalero Tralala, Bombardiro Crocodilo, etc.) that conquered kids' feeds in early 2026.
def.normal: Pure-form AI brain rot that broke containment via Italian-language audio. Deported by the Great Meme Reset.
def.zoomer: Bombardiro Crocodilo is in our hearts. The Reset said no. He returns to the sea.

def.normie: A phone effect that uses AI to make you look unrealistically polished.
def.normal: Polished-to-uncanny AI filter that fuelled a beauty-standards discourse cycle in early 2025. Now fully archived.
def.zoomer: The discourse outlived the filter. Both are gone.

def.normie: An ironic backlash to 'sigma male' content that flooded feeds in early 2026 before collapsing under its own irony.
def.normal: Sigma overload was the meme; the meme killed itself. The irony got too thin to support the format.
def.zoomer: Sigma rest in pieces. Even the irony is cringe now.

def.normie: A playful term from video games — gaining or losing 'aura' for being cool or uncool.
def.normal: Originally sincere; now ironic-only. Sincere use is officially graveyarded.
def.zoomer: Aura only ironically. Sincere aura = -1000 aura.

def.normie: A glitchy, fast electronic music aesthetic that re-emerged in short-form background music in early 2026.
def.normal: Driven by early-2020s nostalgia and faded before the algorithm could fully find it.
def.zoomer: It came back, glitched, left.

def.normie: Intense self-improvement focused on maximising physical attractiveness. Originated in manosphere forums.
def.normal: The peak-mainstream phase ended; the manosphere has moved on to new vocabulary. Term still used but no longer trending.
def.zoomer: Looksmaxxing peaked, mogged itself, exited.

def.normie: A nonsense '6-7' catchphrase chanted by kids and teens, mostly because it's meaningless.
def.normal: The audio meme that broke containment into actual classroom chaos. Officially graveyarded by educators and most users.
def.zoomer: Six seven was the canary in the brain-rot coal mine.

archive.name: Rizz (in any tense)

def.normie: 'Rizz' was Oxford's 2023 word of the year — short for charisma. Now using it sincerely is a dead giveaway you're a millennial.
def.normal: From Kai Cenat to Oxford in two years. Now firmly retired.
def.zoomer: Saying 'rizz' is rizzless. The word killed itself.

archive.vid: rizz oxford word of the year

def.normie: A 2023 trend where creators acted out absurd NPC (video-game character) dialogue trees on live streams.
def.normal: Pre-pandemic-vibes weird internet. Started on Twitch, peaked on TikTok Live, gone.
def.zoomer: NPC livestreams walked so AI-slop streams could run. Iconic, retired.

def.normie: A 2023 viral question: 'How often do men think about the Roman Empire?' Spawned thousands of partner-interview videos.
def.normal: A gender-discourse meme that ate the FYP for two months, then evaporated. Brief mainstream lifespan.
def.zoomer: Roman Empire was the moment your aunt finally posted a meme. Universal canary.

def.normie: A 2023 trend of posting unconventional 'snack-plate' dinners (cheese, crackers, olives, etc.) as 'girl dinners.'
def.normal: Wholesome food meme that opened a discourse about disordered eating, which killed the trend.
def.zoomer: Girl dinner died because the discourse arrived. Plate of cheese and olives is still a vibe though.

def.normie: Pressing your tongue to the roof of your mouth, supposedly to reshape your jawline. From 'looksmaxxing' communities.
def.normal: Peaked as a school-meme shorthand for 'staying silent.' Term still used; the discourse died.
def.zoomer: Mewing is just school silence now. The jawline grift is over.

def.normie: A 2024 catchphrase from creator Jools Lebron about behaving in a modest, reserved way. Brands killed it within weeks.
def.normal: Cultural reset that lasted three weeks before politicians and corporate brands ground it into dust.
def.zoomer: Iconic. Retired. Ironic use only.

battles.q: Which is the meme of the week?
Which World Cup audio outlives the tournament?
Which Stranger Things era is canon?

hot: Stranger Things rap recap meme just merged with Withered Flight — the format of the week
Nyck Caution audio escaped the WC niche and is now soundtracking HOTD S3 fan-edits
Premiere-day discourse flooding every feed — edits to every audio on this board
died: -coded suffix family kept going without it
the badge is just a button now
incoming: analog-horror FlightReacts edits about to fully colonize the FYP — the format pairs with every audio on this board
tip: Mid-week birth, peak-of-the-week energy. Use the audio as POV-narration on any 'announce who you are' edit. Won't survive the weekend unscathed but the next 4 days are gold.
weird: Du bist gut genug × HOTD paradox
German falsetto wholesomeness over Targaryen incest discourse is the cinematic universe nobody asked for and the FYP loves

radar.name: Withered Flight × every audio
radar.sig: The analog-horror FlightReacts edit pairs with all current audios
radar.det: Will Mike rap, Du bist gut genug, PRESSURE — the Withered Flight visual works as the still-image POV for any sound. Format will pollinate everything for two weeks.

radar.name: Clanker court
radar.sig: AI-on-trial skits about to break out
radar.det: Mock-trial videos putting AI tools and bot accounts on trial. The anti-AI wave keeps building.

radar.name: "Algorithm Prefers" format
radar.det: Creators pit a high-effort edit against a deadpan static shot and let the comments vote. Format travels across every niche.

radar.name: HOTD S3 audio remixes
radar.sig: Targaryen edits set to every current audio
radar.det: S3 premiered today; expect a week of dragon-fire edits set to PRESSURE, Du bist gut genug, and Will Mike rap.

radar.name: World Cup own-goal compilations
radar.det: Edits of bewildering refereeing and chaotic VAR moments stitched to PRESSURE and Saxophone Gets Louder.

radar.name: Domestic ASMR sounds
radar.det: Cleaning, packing, morning routines leaning into peel/spray/wipe/tap audio.

radar.name: -coded suffix family
radar.det: Even with 404 coded retired, error-coded, beta-coded, captcha-coded are incubating.

radar.name: Heatwave content
radar.sig: Weather is now driving a content wave
radar.det: European heatwave coverage spawning the familiar summer-discourse cycle.

radar.name: Analog posting
radar.sig: The Reset's aesthetic wing is gaining ground
radar.det: Film photos, unedited clips, deliberately low-tech content as anti-slop signalling.

radar.name: Food jutsu
radar.sig: Cooking videos shot like ninja techniques
radar.det: Stylised, dramatic, lightly absurd food prep clips. Started in food creators' niches, spreading to general FYP.
